raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need to find word hello in regular files under /workspace and display matched file name. Use grep -rl --exclude-dir? Just regular files. Use find to filter regular files. Command: grep -rl --exclude-dir=.git? but not specified. Use grep -rl 'hello' /workspace. That returns file names. But ensure only regular files: grep -rl --exclude-dir=.git? but not necessary. Use find . -type f -exec grep -q 'hello' {} \; -print. But simpler: grep -rl 'hello' /workspace. I'll output that.
